  • Exports

Exception type and helper functions for generating better errors in macro - automatically show original source code (not mangled macro-generated horror you get when mapIt result is passed to function call), support colored multiline annotations for exceptions, multiple annotations on the same line etc.

Imports

colorstring, hseq_mapping, hmath, halgorithm, hseq_distance, clformat, hdebug_misc, base_errors

Procs

proc toColorString(err: CodeError): string {...}{.
    raises: [ValueError, IOError, EOFError], tags: [ReadIOEffect].}
proc assertNodeKind(node: NimNode; kindSet: set[NimNodeKind];
                    iinfo = instantiationInfo()): void {...}{.
    raises: [CodeError, ValueError, IOError, EOFError], tags: [ReadIOEffect].}
assert node kind is in the set. Provide higlighted error with list of expected types and kind of given node 
proc assertNodeKindNot(node: NimNode; kindSet: set[NimNodeKind]; iinfo: LineInfo = (
    filename: "???", line: 0, column: -1).toLineInfo()): void {...}{.
    raises: [CodeError, ValueError, IOError, EOFError], tags: [ReadIOEffect].}
assert node kind is in the set. Provide higlighted error with list of expected types and kind of given node 
proc printSeparator(msg: string): void {...}{.raises: [ValueError],
    tags: [ReadEnvEffect].}
proc getFileName(f: string): string {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}

Funcs

func toLineInfo(arg: InstantiationInfo): LineInfo {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
func startpos(node: NimNode): LineInfo {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
func toCodeError(node: NimNode; message: string; annotation: string = "";
                 lineRange: int = -2; iinfo: LineInfo = LineInfo()): CodeError {...}{.
    raises: [ValueError, IOError, EOFError], tags: [ReadIOEffect].}
func toCodeError(str: string; offset, exprLen: int; message, annotation: string): CodeError {...}{.
    raises: [ValueError, IOError, EOFError], tags: [ReadIOEffect].}
func toCodeError(nodes: openArray[tuple[node: NimNode, annot: string]];
                 message: string; iinfo: LineInfo = (filename: "???", line: 0,
    column: -1).toLineInfo()): CodeError {...}{.
    raises: [ValueError, IOError, EOFError], tags: [ReadIOEffect].}
func toStaticMessage(errpos: LineInfo; expr: string; message: string;
                     annot: string; iinfo: LineInfo = (filename: "???", line: 0,
    column: -1).toLineInfo()): string {...}{.raises: [ValueError, IOError, EOFError],
                                        tags: [ReadIOEffect].}
func toStaticMessage(node: NimNode; message: string; annot: string; iinfo: LineInfo = (
    filename: "???", line: 0, column: -1).toLineInfo()): string {...}{.
    raises: [ValueError, IOError, EOFError], tags: [ReadIOEffect].}
func toStaticMessage(errpos: InstantiationInfo; expr: string; message: string;
                     annot: string): string {...}{.
    raises: [ValueError, IOError, EOFError], tags: [ReadIOEffect].}
func toCompilesAssert(errpos: LineInfo; expr: string; compileBody: NimNode;
                      annotation: string; addBody: bool = true): NimNode {...}{.
    raises: [ValueError, IOError, EOFError], tags: [ReadIOEffect].}
func toCompilesAssert(node, compileBody: NimNode; annotation: string;
                      addBody: bool = false): NimNode {...}{.
    raises: [ValueError, IOError, EOFError], tags: [ReadIOEffect].}

Generate when not compiles() assert for compileBody. If compilation fails show position of the node with annotaion.

This is useful for various DSL that generate code - in that case node is the original code in DSL, compileBody is whatever generated by macro and annotation is additional comment. Something like annotation = "Generated {compileBody.toStrLit()}" is most likely what you want. NOTE: multiline annotations are supported, if generated code is multiline it should not be messed up in output.

func stringMismatchMessage(input: string; expected: openArray[string];
                           colorize: bool = true; fixSuggestion: bool = true;
                           showAll: bool = false): string {...}{.
    raises: [ValueError], tags: [].}
  • TODO :: Better heuristics for missing/extraneous prefix/suffix

Templates

template hxInfo(): untyped {...}{.dirty.}
template staticAssert(assrt: untyped; message, annotation: string;
                      iinfo: InstantiationInfo): untyped
template assertNodeIt(node: NimNode; cond: untyped; msg: untyped;
                      annot: string = ""; iinfo: LineInfo = LineInfo()): untyped
template raiseCodeError(node: NimNode; message: string; annotation: string = "";
                        linerange: int = -2): untyped
template getCEx(t: untyped): untyped
template pprintStackTrace(): untyped
template pprintErr(): untyped
